COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR FILED BY DEFENDANT SHIMEK PROPERTIES DBA TOUCHLESS CAR WASH
ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT SHIMEK PROPERTIES MOTION TO STRIKE

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SHIMEK PROPERTIES DBA TOUCHLESS CAR WASH'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF COMPLAINT IS GRANTED, WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. FOR REQUEST NO. 1, WHETHER PLAINTIFF HAS AN ADEQUATE REMEDY AT LAW IS A QUESTION OF FACT THAT IS NOT PROPER SUBJECT OF A MOTION TO STRIKE. HOWEVER, PARAGRAPH 26(B), PAGE 5, LINES 20-24 IS STRICKEN SINCE INJUNCTIVE RELIEF IS NOT SOUGHT IN PRAYER FOR RELIEF. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO ADD INJUNCTIVE RELIEF TO THE PRAYER FOR RELIEF. FOR REQUEST NOS. 2 - 4 REGARDING PUNITIVE DAMAGES, PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS IN SUPPORT OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARE CONCLUSORY, INSUFFICIENT, AND DO NOT COMPLY WITH CIV. CODE 3294(B). PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO REPLEAD THEM. ORDER SIGNED WITH THE MOVING PAPERS. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-27-2013 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-30-2013 Set For Order To Show Cause On Sep-17-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-25-2013 Continued To Aug-14-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMES DOMBORSKI OF THE LAW OFFICES OF THOMAS SABERI, TEL. NO.
(650) 588-2428 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - DEFENDANT SHIMEK PROPERTIES DBA TOUCHLESS CAR WASH'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF COMPLAINT is Granted, with 10 days leave to amend. For request no. 1, whether Plaintiff has an adequate remedy at law is a question of fact that is not proper subject of a motion to strike. However, paragraph 26(b), page 5, lines 20-24 is stricken since injunctive relief is not sought in prayer for relief. Plaintiff is given leave to add injunctive relief to the prayer for relief. For request nos. 2 - 4 regarding punitive damages, Plaintiff's allegations in support of punitive damages are conclusory, insufficient, and do not comply with Civ. Code 3294(b). Plaintiff is given leave to amend to replead them. ORDER SIGNED WITH THE MOVING PAPERS. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
